frames: The agent starts in a low, crouched posture with all four legs firmly on the checkered ground.
It slightly lowers its central body while the front left leg begins to lift off the ground.
The body continues to descend, and the front left leg extends forward, clearly airborne.
The agent's body is at its lowest point, with the front left leg fully extended forward, poised to land.
The body begins to rise as the front left leg makes contact with the ground, initiating a forward push.
Weight shifts to the front left leg, and the rear right leg lifts off the ground, beginning its forward swing.
The agent moves forward, with the rear right leg swinging through the air, and the body is now higher.
The rear right leg lands, completing a full step, and the agent maintains a stable, forward-leaning posture.
